  and  |  regional  |  dimensions  |  noted  |  above  |  mean  |  that  |  the  |  estimation  |  of
  weights  |  involves  |  a  |  lot  |  more  |  than  |  just  |  the  |  breakdown  |  of
  expenditure  |  by  |  types  |  of  |  goods  |  and  |  services  |  and  |  the  |  number
  of  |  separately  |  weighted  |  indices  |  composing  |  the  |  overall  |  index  |  depends  |  upon
  two  |  factors  |  the  |  degree  |  of  |  detail  |  to  |  which  |  available  |  data
  permit  |  breakdown  |  of  |  total  |  consumption  |  expenditure  |  in  |  the  |  weight  |  by
  type  |  of  |  expenditure  |  region  |  and  |  outlet  |  type  |  whether  |  there  |  is
  reason  |  to  |  believe  |  that  |  price  |  movements  |  vary  |  between  |  these  |  most
  detailed  |  categories  |  how  |  the  |  weights  |  are  |  calculated  |  and  |  in  |  how
  much  |  detail  |  depends  |  upon  |  the  |  availability  |  of  |  information  |  and  |  upon
  the  |  scope  |  of  |  the  |  index  |  in  |  the  |  uk  |  the  |  retail
  price  |  index  |  rpi  |  does  |  not  |  relate  |  to  |  the  |  whole  |  of

  income  |  households  |  whose  |  total  |  household  |  income  |  lies  |  within  |  the  |  top

  can  |  differ  |  between  |  regions  |  and  |  between  |  different  |  types  |  of  |  outlet

  of  |  expenditure  |  for  |  each  |  detailed  |  consumption  |  category  |  for  |  each  |  type

  of  |  food  |  sold  |  in  |  supermarkets  |  the  |  weight  |  for  |  sliced  |  bread

  of  |  weights  |  there  |  are  |  only  |  two  |  general  |  principles  |  for  |  the

  computing  |  an  |  index  |  the  |  weights  |  would  |  represent  |  current  |  annual  |  expenditure
  patterns  |  in  |  practice  |  they  |  necessarily  |  reflect  |  past  |  using  |  the  |  most
  recent  |  data  |  available  |  or  |  if  |  they  |  are  |  not  |  of  |  high

  previous  |  year  |  some  |  countries  |  have  |  used  |  a  |  average  |  in  |  recognition

  the  |  weights  |  for  |  aggregates  |  within  |  aggregates  |  are  |  based  |  on  |  older

  for  |  internet  |  service  |  entered  |  index  |  compilation  |  with  |  a  |  considerable  |  time

  price  |  index  |  has  |  been  |  and  |  remains  |  a  |  subject  |  of  |  heated
  controversy  |  in  |  many  |  countries  |  various  |  approaches  |  have  |  been  |  considered  |  each

  and  |  composition  |  of  |  individuals  |  consumption  |  this  |  standard  |  depends  |  upon  |  the

  or  |  house  |  owned  |  by  |  the  |  consumer  |  who  |  occupies  |  it  |  its

  of  |  thinking  |  an  |  opportunity  |  cost  |  namely  |  what  |  he  |  or  |  she

  opportunity  |  cost  |  can  |  be  |  looked  |  at  |  in  |  two  |  ways  |  since
  there  |  are  |  two  |  alternatives  |  to  |  continuing  |  to  |  live  |  in  |  an

  is  |  to  |  be  |  considered  |  is  |  to  |  sell  |  it  |  earn  |  interest
  on  |  the  |  owner  |  capital  |  thus  |  released  |  and  |  buy  |  it  |  back

  else  |  for  |  the  |  year  |  in  |  which  |  case  |  the  |  cost  |  is
  the  |  rent  |  that  |  could  |  be  |  obtained  |  for  |  it  |  there  |  are
  practical  |  problems  |  in  |  implementing  |  either  |  of  |  these  |  economists  |  approaches  |  thus

  positive  |  once  |  house  |  prices  |  start  |  to  |  fall  |  so  |  such  |  an

  of  |  the  |  consumption  |  of  |  the  |  services  |  of  |  dwellings  |  is  |  included
  in  |  consumption  |  then  |  it  |  must  |  be  |  included  |  in  |  income  |  too

  with  |  dwellings  |  the  |  logic  |  applies  |  equally  |  to  |  all  |  durable  |  consumer

  is  |  like  |  dwellings  |  they  |  yield  |  a  |  consumption  |  service  |  that  |  can

  sole  |  consumer  |  who  |  bought  |  her  |  house  |  in  |  financing  |  half  |  of
  this  |  sum  |  by  |  raising  |  a  |  mortgage  |  the  |  problem  |  is  |  to

  concerns  |  her  |  now  |  a  |  consumer  |  price  |  index  |  compares  |  how  |  much

  in  |  the  |  reference  |  period  |  with  |  what  |  it  |  cost  |  then  |  application
  of  |  the  |  principle  |  thus  |  requires  |  that  |  the  |  index  |  for  |  our
  one  |  house  |  owner  |  reflect  |  the  |  movement  |  of  |  the  |  prices  |  of

  rates  |  if  |  she  |  took  |  out  |  a  |  mortgage  |  it  |  is  |  the

  took  |  out  |  a  |  mortgage  |  it  |  is  |  the  |  change  |  from  |  to
  that  |  counts  |  thus  |  her  |  current  |  index  |  with  |  as  |  a  |  reference
  period  |  will  |  stand  |  at  |  more  |  than  |  if  |  house  |  prices  |  or
  in  |  the  |  case  |  of  |  a  |  mortgage  |  interest  |  rates  |  rose  |  between
  and  |  the  |  application  |  of  |  this  |  principle  |  in  |  the  |  dwellings  |  component
  of  |  a  |  consumer  |  price  |  index  |  is  |  known  |  as  |  the  |  debt
  profile  |  method  |  it  |  means  |  that  |  the  |  current  |  movement  |  of  |  the
  index  |  will  |  reflect  |  past  |  changes  |  in  |  dwelling  |  prices  |  and  |  interest
  rates  |  some  |  people  |  regard  |  this  |  as  |  odd  |  quite  |  a  |  few
  countries  |  use  |  the  |  debt  |  profile  |  method  |  but  |  in  |  doing  |  so
  most  |  of  |  them  |  behave  |  inconsistently  |  consistency  |  would  |  require  |  that  |  the
  index  |  also  |  cover  |  the  |  interest  |  on  |  consumer  |  credit  |  instead  |  of
  the  |  whole  |  price  |  paid  |  for  |  the  |  products  |  bought  |  on  |  credit
  if  |  it  |  covers  |  mortgage  |  interest  |  payments  |  products  |  bought  |  on  |  credit
  would  |  then  |  be  |  treated  |  in  |  the  |  same  |  way  |  as  |  dwellings
  variants  |  of  |  the  |  debt  |  profile  |  method  |  are  |  employed  |  or  |  have
  been  |  proposed  |  one  |  example  |  is  |  to  |  include  |  down  |  payments  |  as
  well  |  as  |  interest  |  another  |  is  |  to  |  correct  |  nominal  |  mortgage  |  rates

  rate  |  of  |  interest  |  also  |  other  |  methods  |  may  |  be  |  used  |  alongside
  the  |  debt  |  profile  |  method  |  thus  |  several  |  countries  |  include  |  a  |  purely
  notional  |  cost  |  of  |  depreciation  |  as  |  an  |  additional  |  index  |  component  |  applying

  both  |  mortgage  |  interest  |  and  |  purchase  |  prices  |  in  |  its  |  index  |  the
  third  |  approach  |  simply  |  treats  |  the  |  acquisition  |  of  |  dwellings  |  in  |  the
  same  |  way  |  as  |  acquisitions  |  of  |  other  |  durable  |  products  |  are  |  treated
  this  |  means  |  taking  |  account  |  of  |  the  |  transaction  |  prices  |  agreed  |  ignoring
  whether  |  payments  |  are  |  delayed  |  or  |  are  |  partly  |  financed  |  by  |  borrowing

  enlarging  |  or  |  reconstructing  |  an  |  dwelling  |  would  |  be  |  covered  |  in  |  addition

  character  |  are  |  advanced  |  in  |  connection  |  with  |  this  |  transactional  |  approach  |  one

  enter  |  a  |  consumption  |  price  |  index  |  it  |  is  |  said  |  that  |  this

  example  |  it  |  may  |  be  |  thought  |  to  |  help  |  understand  |  and  |  facilitate
